name: Federal Information Security Modernization Act
owner: US Office of Management and Budget / CISA
description: US federal statute requiring agencies to develop, document, and implement programs to secure federal information and information systems. Original FISMA (2002, Pub. L. 107-347 Title III) was amended by the Federal Information Security Modernization Act of 2014 (Pub. L. 113-283), which clarified responsibilities of OMB, DHS (now CISA), and agency heads.
relationToCloudSecurity: FISMA is the basis for FedRAMP — cloud service providers serving federal agencies operate under FedRAMP authorization, which is FISMA-compliant by construction. Mandates use of NIST standards (SP 800-53, FIPS 199/200) for federal systems.
relationToAISecurity: AI systems operated by or for US federal agencies fall under FISMA scope. OMB Memo M-24-10 (March 2024) provides AI-specific implementation guidance overlaying the FISMA framework.

license.notes: US federal statutes are public-domain government works (17 USC §105) and may be freely redistributed.

lifecycle.notes: FISMA 2014 (Pub. L. 113-283) is current. Annual agency reports to OMB and Congress.

current_extraction.notes: Parsed from marker-extracted PLAW markdown via extract_us_plaw.py. Found 11 SEC./§ markers.

extraction_history.reason: Basic PLAW section extraction from marker markdown

notes: Foundational US federal-agency information security statute. Acquire PLAW-113publ283 text for structured extraction.
